evals.name: warden-drift-fr-de
evals.prompt: Run a translation consistency check comparing evals/fixtures/warden-drift/source-en.md against the fr-FR and de-DE translations in the same folder. This is Warden Security content — the project has Warden's brand and localization guides under clients/warden/.
evals.expected_output: Two separate reports: per-translation compliance (fr-FR has 3 findings, de-DE is clean) and cross-translation drift (4 findings: CPU stat mismatch, missing de-DE paragraph, fr-FR formality switch, fr-FR internal term inconsistency). See answerkey.md for full detail.

assertions.text: Report 1 (fr-FR) flags 'protection instantanée' as inconsistent with the approved 'protection en temps réel'
Report 1 (fr-FR) flags 'tableau de bord' — per the fr-FR locale note, 'Dashboard' should stay in English in tech contexts
Report 1 (fr-FR) flags 'tu verras' as a formality/register violation (fr-FR defaults to vous)
Report 1 (de-DE) has zero or at most 1 minor finding — the translation is compliant
Report 2 flags the CPU impact statistic mismatch (source says under 2%, fr-FR says 5%) and names fr-FR as the affected locale
Report 2 flags the missing closing paragraph in de-DE as structural drift, naming de-DE as the affected locale
Report 2 flags the fr-FR internal formality inconsistency (vous vs. tu) as register drift
Report 2 flags the 'real-time protection' term inconsistency within fr-FR, and contrasts it with de-DE's consistent 'Echtzeitschutz'
Output contains two clearly separate, labeled reports/tables (compliance and drift), not one merged table
Every Report 2 finding names which specific locale(s) are affected
The summary or findings explicitly call out the CPU number mismatch as a high-severity / claims-discipline issue, not just a minor style note

evals.name: warden-clean-false-positive-control
evals.prompt: Run a translation consistency check comparing evals/fixtures/warden-clean/source-en.md against the fr-FR and de-DE translations in the same folder. This is Warden Security content — the project has Warden's brand and localization guides under clients/warden/.
evals.expected_output: Both reports come back empty or near-empty — terminology, register, numeric claims (none present), and structure are all consistent across languages.

assertions.text: Report 1 has zero or at most 1 minor finding across both locales
Report 2 has zero or at most 1 minor finding
Output does not claim a terminology inconsistency exists (fr-FR and de-DE both use their approved, consistent terms)
Output still presents two separate reports even though both are empty/near-empty, rather than collapsing into one
